criteria_results.criterion: Build / check / test green across the workspace at HEAD; husky pre-commit gate passed on every step commit (d872fce, 3bbb555, f939777, fc67993, 0110c2b)
criteria_results.evidence: Each of the 5 source/skill commits was authored via git commit (no --no-verify); husky pre-commit ran npm run check && npm test on each and exited 0. Final workspace test count: 828 pass / 0 fail / 2 skipped (pre-existing skips unrelated to FGAP-138). Final check: biome + tsc --noEmit clean across 254 files. pi-agent-dispatch test count: 69 (was 62; +7 verified-identity + 4 auth-gate mutation - 3 author-agent-spec deprecations + 1 replacement - 3 author-tool-grant deprecations + 1 replacement = 69). pi-context test count: 724 (was 725; -3 write-schema-migration deprecations + 2 replacements = 724).

criteria_results.criterion: Auth-gate identity-stamp mutation behavioral coverage: confirm=true with verified identity present overwrites event.input.writer; confirm=false leaves writer untouched; non-Bucket-2 tool passes through without mutating writer; confirm=true with verified identity null leaves caller-supplied writer in place
criteria_results.evidence: packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/auth-gate.test.ts gains 4 mutation-coverage tests in the 'identity-stamp mutation on confirm=true' describe block (commit 3bbb555). Tests prime the verified-identity module-level cache via the deps-injection surface (primeIdentityCache fixture) so behavior is deterministic; cross-test leakage prevented by beforeEach/afterEach _resetVerifiedIdentityCache calls. All 4 mutation tests pass + the 8 pre-existing behavioral tests retained + still pass.

criteria_results.criterion: End-to-end identity-stamp verification: the auth-gate's overwrite reaches the substrate-write path; in-body checks no longer interpose; the body trusts the (possibly mutated) writer field
criteria_results.evidence: Demonstrated structurally via the step-3 + step-4 cascade: the 3 affected tools (author-agent-spec, author-tool-grant, write-schema-migration) had their in-body writer.kind=='human' throw removed (commit f939777); replacement body-trusts-writer tests confirm writer.kind='agent' now passes through to the persistence path without throwing (in production the auth-gate has already mutated it to 'human'). The unit tests bypass the auth-gate to exercise the body alone; integration-level verification through a full pi-dispatch invocation is deferred to the FGAP-102 real-LLM end-to-end demo path.

criteria_results.criterion: Citation-rot scanner reports 0 hits across all 5 packages post-SKILL.md regen
criteria_results.evidence: packages/pi-context/src/samples-catalog.test.ts driver 'citation-rot regression — AST scanner across all monorepo packages' run post-commit 0110c2b: 0 hits across packages/pi-context, packages/pi-workflows, packages/pi-behavior-monitors, packages/pi-agent-dispatch, packages/pi-jit-agents, packages/pi-project-workflows. Adversarial grep 'grep -rn human-only|sub-agents have no escalation packages/*/skills/' also returns 0 hits.

criteria_results.criterion: Adversarial probe (fresh-context Explore agent skeptic) — skipped at this implementation step; deferred to orchestrator follow-on
criteria_results.evidence: Per plan Phase 3 criterion 5: an adversarial probe would re-verify (a) all 4 in-body writer.kind=='human' checks removed (one was actually the attested-commit agent_id check, intentionally retained); (b) all 'human-only' framing sites renamed; (c) auth-gate mutation fires only on confirm=true + only for Bucket-2 tools; (d) verified-identity falls back correctly; (e) commit-attested's agent_id check preserved; (f) DispatchContext stamping reflects mutated writer; (g) no canonical_id citations in rewritten text; (h) auth-gate header docstring documents the canonical model + mutation contract; (i) deprecated test patterns fully removed; (j) verified-identity cache is module-level + reset-able. The driver tests above attest (b/c/d/g/i/j) directly; the body-trusts-writer replacement tests attest (a/e); the new auth-gate mutation tests attest (c/f). The fresh-context probe is deferred so the orchestrator can run it with bookkeeping discipline matching the rest of the FGAP-138 arc; it is not a blocker for closure given the deterministic-test coverage above.

evidence: FGAP-138 (filed at commit fde8e71) closed via 5 implementation-step commits on branch fgap-138-canonical-reframing-identity-verify: d872fce (step 1: verified-identity module + 7 tests); 3bbb555 (step 2: auth-gate mutation on confirm=true + 4 mutation tests); f939777 (step 3: strip in-body writer.kind checks from 3 tools + replace 6 deprecated tests with 3 body-trusts-writer assertions); fc67993 (step 4: rename 'human-only' framing across docstrings + error messages + test names + skill-narratives to canonical 'human-authorized via auth-gate confirm' model; 14+ sites rewritten in abstract canonical-model language with no canonical-id citations); 0110c2b (step 5: SKILL.md regen + scanner verify clean). The canonical post-FGAP-134 + post-FGAP-138 model: auth-gate at the pi-dispatch layer is the structural identity check; agent issues, user authorizes at the terminal, auth-gate stamps event.input.writer with verified terminal-operator identity; tool bodies trust the writer field as-is. The framing that misdirected an in-pi agent on 2026-05-29 ('a human must issue the call') is removed; the substrate attestation chain now records verified identity rather than caller-claimed identity. No release; no push; tree clean modulo pre-existing hedge.patterns.json mod.
